history.qwen_observation: Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade switched dependencies to Jakarta EE 10 and Spring Security 6, but source code still references javax.* imports and deprecated Spring Security 5 APIs, causing immediate compilation failure.
history.qwen_rationale: Combining UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3 with explicit Jakarta migration recipes and SpringBoot3BestPractices in a single step ensures all namespace and API changes are applied before the first compilation check, preventing early chain abort.

history.qwen_observation: The build failed during the Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade step because the `hibernate5ddl-maven-plugin` (a Hibernate 5 plugin) is incompatible with the Hibernate 6 dependencies introduced by the Spring Boot 3 upgrade, causing a runtime exception during the DDL generation phase of the compile lifecycle.
history.qwen_rationale: Spring Boot 3.x requires Hibernate 6, but the project uses `de.jpdigital.maven.plugins:hibernate5ddl-maven-plugin`, which is designed for Hibernate 5. This plugin runs during the compile phase and fails when it encounters Hibernate 6 classes or configurations. To fix this, we must remove the incompatible plugin before attempting the Spring Boot upgrade. We will use a POM_PATCH step to remove the plugin, then proceed with the Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade and Jakarta migration.

history.qwen_observation: The hibernate5ddl-maven-plugin executes during mvn compile and fails because it relies on Hibernate 5 APIs, which are incompatible with the Hibernate 6 dependencies introduced by Spring Boot 3.3.
history.qwen_rationale: Removing the incompatible Maven plugin before the Spring Boot upgrade prevents the build failure during the recipe application phase. The chain then proceeds with the Spring Boot 3.3 and Jakarta EE 10 migrations, followed by the standard Java 21 plugin and build upgrades, and finally Java 21 code transforms.

history.qwen_observation: The hibernate5ddl-maven-plugin fails during mvn compile after Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade because it is incompatible with Hibernate 6 and attempts to connect to an unconfigured database.
history.qwen_rationale: Removing the incompatible hibernate5ddl-maven-plugin before applying the Spring Boot 3.3 and Jakarta migration recipes prevents the plugin from executing during the build, allowing the framework upgrade to proceed without database connection errors.
